summary: VitaDAO funding proposal (VDP-63) for Matrix Bio, a venture with the Gorbunova Lab developing small molecule hyaluronidase inhibitors to increase high molecular weight hyaluronic acid (HMW-HA) levels for cancer treatment and longevity. The proposal outlines a $300k budget for high-throughput screening and validation studies based on naked mole rat research showing cancer resistance from HMW-HA.

content_blocks.title: One-liner and Team
content_blocks.content: VitaDAO and Gorbunova Lab launching Matrix Bio to leverage anti-cancer and pro-longevity effects of high molecular weight hyaluronic acid from naked mole rat to human. Scientific evaluation by Sebastian Brunemeier, Tim Peterson, Rhys Anderson, Koen De Lombaert. Project PI: Vera Gorbunova, Professor at University of Rochester.

content_blocks.title: Simple Summary - Scientific Rationale
content_blocks.content: Naked mole rats (NMR) live up to 40 years vs 3 years for normal rats and are cancer resistant. Gorbunova lab demonstrated NMR cancer resistance is mediated by high molecular weight hyaluronic acid (HMW-HA) in tissues (Reference 1). Transgenic mice expressing naked mole rat hyaluronan synthase gene (NHAS2) showed less tumours, improved health, and 10% longer lifespan (Reference 2). Project will screen and develop small molecule inhibitors of hyaluronidases to increase HA in human patients.

content_blocks.title: Problem Statement - Medical Need
content_blocks.content: Aging is major risk factor for diseases including cancer. Cancer caused 10 million deaths worldwide and 19.3 million new cases in 2020. Tumor microenvironment plays critical role including extracellular matrix (ECM) components. HA role in tumor depends on polymer size: LMW-HA promotes neo-angiogenesis, tumor cell migration, invasion, and proliferation through CD44 interactions.

content_blocks.title: Opportunity - HMW-HA Mechanism
content_blocks.content: HMW-HA promotes tissue homeostasis and prevents tissue metastasis through CD44 interactions and signaling. Very-high-molecular-weight HA (vHMW-HA) has superior cytoprotective properties vs shorter HMW-HA, protecting human and mouse cells from stress-induced cell-cycle arrest and cell death (Reference 7). Transgenic mice expressing NMR hyaluronan synthase gene have less cancer, improved health, and 10% longer lifespan (Reference 2). Strategy: increase HMW-HA and decrease LMW-HA by inhibiting hyaluronidases.

content_blocks.title: IP Roadmap
content_blocks.content: Gorbunova lab has designed complex validating functional screening assay for hyaluronidase inhibitors. Previous ~3k compound screen identified one natural product that increases HA levels in mouse and human tissues. IP will include formulations/modifications of this natural product. Plan: larger high throughput screen (HTS) with proprietary hyaluronidase inhibition assay and medicinal chemistry optimization of hits to produce patentable new chemical entities. Two targets identified with one prioritized (not disclosed for IP protection).

content_blocks.title: Budget Allocation
content_blocks.content: Total funding: up to $300k from VitaDAO. Allocation: up to $200k for HTS and exploratory medicinal chemistry with 3rd party CRO or academic fee-for-service facility; up to $100k to Gorbunova lab for hit compound experiments; $5k/month consulting agreement. Program 1 (HTS and med chem for new inhibitors): Phase 1 HTS up to $100k, Phase 2 medicinal chemistry up to $100k. Program 2 (validation of existing in vivo active inhibitor with EC50 ~20 µM): administration to mouse cancer models testing curative and preventative effects, up to $100k.

content_blocks.title: Detailed Budget Items
content_blocks.content: Funds cover: technician effort, mouse cost, chemical cost, culturing human cancer cell lines for xenografts, mouse evaluation for tumor burden. Milestones to be agreed in separate full R&D plan.

content_blocks.title: Principal Investigator - Vera Gorbunova
content_blocks.content: PhD, endowed Professor of Biology and Medicine at University of Rochester, co-director of Rochester Aging Research Center. Research focuses on longevity mechanisms and exceptionally long-lived mammals. Pioneered comparative biology approach to study aging. Elucidated tumor suppressor evolution mechanisms. Uncovered Sirtuin 6 function in genome stability. Awards from Ellison Medical Foundation, Glenn Foundation, AFAR, NIH. Prizes: Cozzarelli Prize from PNAS, ADPS/Allianz prize for aging research, Prince Hitachi Prize in Comparative Oncology, Davey Prize from Wilmot Cancer Center.

content_blocks.title: Highlights
content_blocks.content: Novel idea from world-leader with extensive longevity/healthspan experience. Strong published supporting evidence for target. May work against many cancer types - high impact. May address other ECM and skin-related conditions: wound healing, arthritis, joint conditions. Validated tool compound identified showing target is druggable. Strong IP potential with possible novel chemical matter.

content_blocks.title: Risks
content_blocks.content: Hyaluronidase inhibition may not be effective for health improvement - most clinical hyaluronic acid data limited to dermatology (skin aging), unclear if works systemically to increase life/healthspan and reduce cancer in humans. Very novel therefore high-risk. Mixed literature evidence on hyaluronan role in metastasis. Authors previously published (Takasugi et al., 2020) that several cytoprotective effects of HA driven specifically by vHMW-HA (>6MDa), which may depend on two naked mole rat-unique amino acid variations in HAS2 active site, thus may not be producible in human cells even with hyaluronidase inhibition. Potential impact on chemotherapy and immunotherapy efficacy if taken in combination.

content_blocks.title: Senior Review - Qualitative Evaluation
content_blocks.content: Reviewed by 8 senior reviewers (scientists, biotech business experts, venture capitalist, MD). PI is world leader in comparative biology focused on longevity/healthspan. Unique approach with little competition on mechanism of action, straightforward road to NCE IP. Strong published supporting evidence for target in mice. Cancer prevention mechanism well established via CD44. Already has screen hit with potential asset. First DeSci project where DAO spins out company. However, hyaluronan role in cancer somewhat controversial: HMW-HA production linked to cancer resistance but also involved in driving/maintaining malignant progression. Hypothesis needs more PoC data. Unclear if intervention works systemically in humans. No detailed plans presented. Early and very high risk, but no insurmountable weaknesses.

content_blocks.title: Senior Review - Quantitative Scores
content_blocks.content: Reviewer scores (scale 1-5, 5 highest): Novelty: 4.3, Feasibility & Data: 3.5, Relevance: 4.6, Science Team: 4.6, Market Advantage: 3.5, IP Potential: 3.7, Conviction score: 4.3

content_blocks.title: Key References
content_blocks.content: Reference 1 (Tian et al. Nature 2013): High-molecular-mass hyaluronan mediates cancer resistance of naked mole rat. Reference 2 (Zhang et al. 2022 preprint): Naked Mole-Rat Hyaluronan Synthase 2 promotes longevity and enhances healthspan in mice. Reference 7 (Takasugi et al. Nat Commun 2020): Naked mole-rat very-high-molecular-mass hyaluronan exhibits superior cytoprotective properties.

key_findings: Transgenic mice expressing NMR NHAS2 gene show 10% lifespan increase, less tumors, improved health - establishes proof-of-concept in mammalian model (TRL 2-3)
Existing tool compound identified with EC50 ~20 µM showing in vivo activity - demonstrates target druggability
Previous ~3k compound screen identified natural product that increases HA levels in mouse and human tissues - early lead matter exists
Proprietary functional screening assay for hyaluronidase inhibition developed and validated
Project stage: Early discovery - planning HTS campaign and medicinal chemistry optimization, no lead compounds optimized yet (TRL 1-2)
Budget: $300k total - $200k for HTS and medicinal chemistry at CRO, $100k for mouse xenograft validation studies
Key risk: vHMW-HA cytoprotective effects may require NMR-specific HAS2 amino acid variations not present in humans, limiting translatability
IP strategy: formulations/modifications of natural product hit, plus new chemical entities from HTS campaign
No detailed R&D plan, milestones, or timeline provided in proposal
Senior reviewer scores indicate high novelty (4.3) and relevance (4.6) but moderate feasibility/data (3.5) reflecting early stage
